---
title: "SCT"
subtitle: "Run SCTransform with **no** covariate regressed"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

We build a baseline single-cell clustering for the microglia_switch dataset. We apply `SCTransform` without regressing out any covariate, then run the standard graph-based clustering pipeline (PCA → KNN → Louvain). 





```{r}
#| label: setup

library(Seurat)
library(tidyverse)
library(qs2)
library(fs)
# scCustomize only used here for its categorical palette helper on UMAPs.
library(scCustomize)

library(kableExtra)

source("R/20260217-helper_functions.R")


# Seed Louvain (and any future stochastic step) so cluster labels stay stable across renders.
set.seed(1)

# load the post-SCT already saved
xen <- qs_read("data/20260512_microglia_switch_sct.qs2")

```

## SCTransform (no covariate regressed)

```{r}
#| label: sct
#| eval: false
# eval:false because this block is the one-off build step; the checkpoint above is reloaded on every render.

# Load the raw merged object produced by R/20260512_xenium2seurat.R.
xen <- qs_read("data/20260512_microglia_switch_original_seurat.qs2")
# Xenium is the count assay; force it as default so SCTransform reads the right matrix.
DefaultAssay(xen) <- "Xenium"
cat("cells:",    ncol(xen),
    " features:", nrow(xen),
    " assays:",  paste(Assays(xen), collapse = ", "),
    "\n")

# Baseline normalization with no covariate regressed
xen <- SCTransform(xen, assay = "Xenium", verbose = FALSE)
# Switch default to SCT so downstream Run/Find* use the residuals, not raw counts.
DefaultAssay(xen) <- "SCT"
cat("variable features:", length(VariableFeatures(xen)), "\n")

# Default npcs (50) is fine; we only use the first 30 downstream.
xen <- RunPCA(xen)

# 1:30 PCs matches the QC plan; FindNeighbors default k = 20.
xen <- FindNeighbors(xen, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)

# algorithm = 1 is plain Louvain (not Leiden); resolution 0.5 is the agreed coarse-grain setting.
xen <- FindClusters(xen, resolution = 0.5, algorithm = 1, verbose = FALSE)

# UMAP only for visual QC of the clusters; not used by any downstream stat.
xen <- RunUMAP(xen, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)

# qs_save(xen, "data/20260512_microglia_switch_sct.qs2")
```

## Plot PCA

```{r}
#| label: palette
colorines <- scCustomize_Palette(num_groups = n_distinct(xen$seurat_clusters), ggplot_default_colors = FALSE)
```

```{r}
#| label: pca
# Split by slide to eyeball the slide effect on PC1/PC2 before any correction
PCAPlot(xen,split.by = "slide", cols = colorines)
```

## Plot UMAP

```{r}
#| label: umap
# Palette sized to the actual cluster count so colours stay stable if resolution changes the number of clusters.

DimPlot(xen, cols = colorines,label = TRUE,label.size = 5) + NoLegend()

```

### Per slide
 
```{r}
#| label: umap-slide
#| fig-width: 10
#| column: page

# Side-by-side UMAPs per slide 
 DimPlot(xen, split.by = "slide",  cols = colorines)+ NoLegend()

```

### Per sample

```{r}
#| label: umap-sample
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 12
#| column: page

 # Per-sample facets check whether 4s2M_F2 (partial section) lands in the same cluster geometry as the other six.
 DimPlot(xen, split.by = "sample_id", ncol = 3, cols = colorines)+ NoLegend()


```

## Find all markers


```{r}
#| label: find-all-markers
#| eval: false

# devtools::install_github('immunogenomics/presto')
library(presto) # faster implementation of the Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test

Idents(xen) <- "seurat_clusters"
sct_markers <- FindAllMarkers(xen,only.pos = TRUE, densify = TRUE)

# write_csv(sct_markers, file = "results/20260514-find_all_markers_cst_noRegressed.csv")
```

 
```{r}
 #| label: markers-table

sct_markers <- read_csv("results/20260514-find_all_markers_cst_noRegressed.csv")

# Top 10 markers per cluster by avg_log2FC
sct_markers <- sct_markers |> 
  select(cluster, gene, avg_log2FC, p_val_adj) |> 
  group_by(cluster) |>
  arrange(desc(avg_log2FC), .by_group = TRUE)
  
  
kbl(sct_markers |> slice_head(n = 10)) |> 
  kable_styling("striped") |> 
  scroll_box(height = "500px")
  
```

```{r}
#| label: markers-violin
#| column: page
#| fig-height: 25
#| fig-width: 12

top_5_genes_sct <- sct_markers |> 
  slice_head(n = 5) |>
  pull(gene) |>
  unique()

fiddle(xen,
       genes_of_interest = top_5_genes_sct,
       classification_col = "seurat_clusters",
       assay = "SCT")
```
